question: How do you enforce boundaries between plugins to avoid cross-dependencies?
answer: I like having plugins to give some forced separation. I make sure my plugins don't depend on each other; a feature plugin shouldn't reference another plugin. If someone tries to create such a dependency, I can catch that early with tests and prevent it during development.

question: Why do you keep a very thin entry point in your applications?
answer: I keep the entry point extremely thin so I can swap it out and reuse the same code behind it. For example, I started with a console/terminal entry point, then swapped to an MCP server to host an LLM harness, keeping almost all of the code unchanged.

question: What pattern do you use to manage heavy dependencies in plugin features?
answer: I use an adapter pattern with inversion of control. If a feature depends on a heavy dependency, I provide an adapter project that brings in the real implementation; otherwise I ship a default/no-op adapter. This lets you opt into the heavy dependency only when needed and keeps the rest of the system lightweight.
